title: daphne orientalis
intro: Daphne orientalis, also known as the spurge laurel, is a flowering evergreen shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It contains daphnin, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Daphne orientalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antiseptic. Daphne orientalis is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although its toxic compounds require careful handling and dosing to avoid adverse effects.
